title: staged image pull with composefs flaky
body:
In the new GH actions based CI the the system tests flake on fedora-rawhide when using the remote tests.

I doubt it is related to rawhide it is just that we only enable composefs there. The following failures have been observed:

```
not ok 460 |700| podman play with image volume pull policies in 1740ms
# tags: ci:parallel
# (from function `bail-now' in file test/system/helpers.bash, line 230,
#  from function `die' in file test/system/helpers.bash, line 967,
#  from function `run_podman' in file test/system/helpers.bash, line 608,
#  in test file test/system/700-play.bats, line 1217)
#   `run_podman pull $volimg_both' failed
#
# [12:12:18.971869413] # /var/tmp/podman/bin/podman-remote --url=unix:///tmp/bats-run-NytU1N/suite/remotesystem.podman.swAQJu.sock pull quay.io/libpod/alpine:latest
# [12:12:19.231668464] Error: unable to copy from source docker://quay.io/libpod/alpine:latest: copying system image from manifest list: partial pull of blob sha256:9d16cba9fb961d1aafec9542f2bf7cb64acfc55245f9e4eb5abecd4cdc38d749: staging a partially-pulled layer: failed to measure verity for "/83/2cd0f75b227d13aac82b1f70b7f90191a4186c151f9db50851d209c45ede11": no data available
# [12:12:19.235752750] [ rc=125 (** EXPECTED 0 **) ]
# #/vvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvv
# #| FAIL: exit code is 125; expected 0
# #\^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
# # [teardown]
```
```
 not ok 460 |700| podman play with image volume pull policies in 3488ms
# tags: ci:parallel
# (from function `bail-now' in file test/system/helpers.bash, line 230,
#  from function `die' in file test/system/helpers.bash, line 967,
#  from function `run_podman' in file test/system/helpers.bash, line 608,
#  in test file test/system/700-play.bats, line 1218)
#   `run_podman run --name $ctrname $volimg_both sh -c "touch /$localfile"' failed
#
# [20:09:07.261747022] # /var/tmp/podman/bin/podman-remote --url=unix:///tmp/bats-run-Oxs8vD/suite/remotesystem.podman.7VfsbV.sock pull quay.io/libpod/alpine:latest
# [20:09:07.755580708] Trying to pull quay.io/libpod/alpine:latest...
# Getting image source signatures
# Copying blob sha256:9d16cba9fb961d1aafec9542f2bf7cb64acfc55245f9e4eb5abecd4cdc38d749
# Copying config sha256:961769676411f082461f9ef46626dd7a2d1e2b2a38e6a44364bcbecf51e66dd4
# Writing manifest to image destination
# 961769676411f082461f9ef46626dd7a2d1e2b2a38e6a44364bcbecf51e66dd4
#
# [20:09:07.766056523] # /var/tmp/podman/bin/podman-remote --url=unix:///tmp/bats-run-Oxs8vD/suite/remotesystem.podman.7VfsbV.sock run --name c-t460-fsptppzk quay.io/libpod/alpine:latest sh -c touch /localfile
# [20:09:09.002618134] {"msg":"exec container process (missing dynamic library?) `/bin/sh`: No such file or directory","level":"error","time":"2026-05-27T20:09:08.972706Z"}
# [20:09:09.007692432] [ rc=1 (** EXPECTED 0 **) ]
# #/vvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvv
# #| FAIL: exit code is 1; expected 0
# #\^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
# # [teardown]
```
```
not ok 197 |200| podman pod create should fail when infra-name is already in use in 1593ms
# tags: ci:parallel
# (from function `bail-now' in file test/system/helpers.bash, line 230,
#  from function `die' in file test/system/helpers.bash, line 967,
#  from function `run_podman' in file test/system/helpers.bash, line 608,
#  in test file test/system/200-pod.bats, line 369)
#   `run_podman --noout pod create --name $pod_name --infra-name "$infra_name" --infra-image "$infra_image"' failed
#
# [18:01:27.475874360] # /var/tmp/podman/bin/podman-remote --url=unix:///tmp/bats-run-EUEsW0/suite/remotesystem.podman.mkBRI8.sock --noout pod create --name p-t197-9nul2vzf --infra-name infra_container_t197-9nul2vzf --infra-image quay.io/libpod/k8s-pause:3.5
# [18:01:27.599323885] Error: failed to make pod: unable to copy from source docker://quay.io/libpod/k8s-pause:3.5: copying system image from manifest list: partial pull of blob sha256:019d8da33d911d9baabe58ad63dea2107ed15115cca0fc27fc0f627e82a695c1: staging a partially-pulled layer: failed to measure verity for "/93/27b07c20ed4058efc79ac5696db4d14d43c859f6bb3e734b4b0a71353f0309": no data available
# [18:01:27.603407413] [ rc=125 (** EXPECTED 0 **) ]
# #/vvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvv
# #| FAIL: exit code is 125; expected 0
# #\^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
# # [teardown]
```

All on remote tests only, the reason we did not see this in cirrus us because we mount a tmpfs as backinf store there where fsverity is not supported.
